body_txt: This trope is what happens when a character loses an old power/ability/stat , but obtains a new one in exchange permanently. It's commonly found in stories where heroes or villains gain New Powers as the Plot Demands by sacrificing something. In Video Games , this often happens when a character acquires a powerup that gives them a stat boost in exchange for losing strength in other areas. Your Disc-One Nuke gets a powerup that allows it to carry you to the end of the game, but your character loses health and must keep themself healed via potions or other Applied Phlebotinum , for example. In Real-Time Strategy games, an upgrade may give your faction a boost in attack at the expense of health , or cheap/fast training units at the cost of losing some attack or armor . For instances where you literally discard and draw cards, see Card Cycling . Power Returned Stronger is a subtrope for when a power gained is similar to a previous one, but stronger. Compare Re-Power , which also involves a character getting a new powerset, but as part of a re-imagining or Alternate Universe and Ability Rewrite , in which an ability is altered as a result of some other item or ability, and isn't always permanent. Can result in Power-Up Letdown if the replacement doesn't live up to the lost skill. A subset of Powers as Programs . Supertrope to Mutually Exclusive Power-Ups , a gameplay mechanic version of this.
